projects.title: Pulse
projects.description: An open-source uptime and cron monitoring platform. Track HTTP endpoints, monitor background jobs, route alerts, and publish public status pages.
projects.extended_description: Built as a comprehensive monitoring solution for the Dhanur ecosystem. Features concurrent active pinging, passive cron heartbeats, robust alerting, and customizable public status pages.
highlights: Active HTTP monitors and passive cron heartbeats
Alert routing via Webhooks or Telegram

projects.title: Authy
projects.description: Centralized single sign-on (SSO) and JWT authentication service for the Dhanur ecosystem.
projects.extended_description: Provides secure identity management and token issuance across all *.dhanur.me applications. Built to centralize auth logic and reduce redundancy in sibling services.

projects.title: Up
projects.description: Real-time service health dashboard for all dhanur.me services. Written in Go, zero external dependencies, deployed on Render.
projects.extended_description: A lightweight status monitor with concurrent goroutine probes and in-memory rolling history. Designed for absolute minimal resource footprint.
highlights: Concurrent goroutines probe every service every 30 seconds
Pure Go standard library with zero external dependencies
Single embedded HTML file served at root

projects.title: Face Rekognition
projects.description: IoT-powered automated attendance system using ESP32-CAM, AWS IoT Core, Lambda, and AWS Rekognition. Serverless architecture with face detection and real-time processing.
projects.extended_description: Built as an academic production-grade prototype, this project focused on low-latency recognition events, robust event ingestion, and auditable attendance records across unreliable network conditions.
projects.problem: Manual attendance workflows are error-prone and slow, especially in classrooms where time-to-start matters.
projects.approach: Combined edge camera capture with a serverless event pipeline. Recognition events flow through AWS IoT and Lambda, then persist to DynamoDB with timestamped logs for traceability.
projects.outcome: Delivered a working end-to-end attendance flow with near real-time updates, reproducible infra behavior, and clear monitoring points for failure analysis.
highlights: ESP32-CAM powered capture at the edge
Event-driven backend with AWS IoT Core and Lambda
Serverless architecture optimized for low ops overhead

projects.title: Beat.pe
projects.description: A web-based rhythm game where timing is everything. Built entirely from scratch with vanilla JavaScript and CSS animations.
projects.extended_description: This was a deliberate exercise in browser performance, deterministic timing, and responsive game UX without framework abstractions.
projects.problem: Most rhythm game prototypes feel laggy on low-end laptops because input handling and animation updates drift over time.
projects.approach: Implemented frame-aware timing windows, stateful input handling, and lightweight animation orchestration using vanilla JavaScript and CSS.
projects.outcome: Shipped a smooth browser game with stable interactions, responsive layout behavior, and fast initial load for casual play sessions.
highlights: Responsive layout tuned for laptop and mobile

projects.title: Listly
projects.description: Terminal-based music playlist manager built from scratch using custom doubly linked lists. Features persistent file storage and an ANSI-colored CLI.
projects.extended_description: Listly was designed as a data-structure-first project to practice writing maintainable core logic before polishing interface ergonomics.
highlights: Persistent storage via plain file I/O
Clear separation between model and command actions

projects.title: dhanur.me
projects.description: The site you're looking at right now. Fast, static, and occasionally broken when I push to main at 3 AM.
projects.extended_description: This portfolio is built to stay lightweight while still supporting search, taxonomy navigation, performance-first rendering, and maintainable content generation workflows.
highlights: Zola static architecture with data-driven content generation
Unified taxonomy system across blog, about, and projects
Tailwind + DaisyUI component styling with custom polish
Automated project page sync integrated into build pipeline

projects.title: Tasks
projects.description: A production-ready RESTful API for managing tasks, built with Spring Boot 3.5.10 and Java 21.
projects.extended_description: Built as a production-grade demonstration of the centralized Dhanur UI component system. Incorporates seamless theming, robust keyboard shortcuts, and local workflow persistence.

projects.title: Linkr
projects.description: A minimal, secure, and intelligent URL shortener built with Rust and Supabase.
projects.extended_description: Linkr provides robust sub-link routing for my personal domains. Features a slick UI built top to bottom with the new Dhanur UI component library.
highlights: Security hardened with SSRF protection and CSP

projects.title: stuff
projects.description: A whole lot of personal utility tools and experiments bundled in one repo.
projects.extended_description: This is my practical junk drawer: tiny CLIs, helper scripts, and quality-of-life automations I keep building whenever something feels repetitive.
highlights: Personal tools focused on daily workflow friction
Small utilities designed for fast iteration
Actively evolving with new scripts over time

projects.title: LeetCode Solutions
projects.description: A structured collection of algorithmic problem-solving approaches and optimizations.
projects.extended_description: Maintained as an algorithm notebook focused on patterns, edge-case handling, and complexity tradeoff documentation.

projects.title: Java DSA
projects.description: From-scratch implementations of fundamental data structures and classic algorithms.
projects.extended_description: This repository serves as a fundamentals lab to keep core CS concepts fresh and implementation quality disciplined.
highlights: Focus on clean, readable Java patterns

projects.title: Selenium Automata
projects.description: Web automation scripts for exploring browser workflows and routine UI-driven tasks.
projects.extended_description: Created to test repeatable browser workflows and sharpen reliability instincts around UI automation.

projects.title: Tic-Tac-Toe
projects.description: Because every developer has to build one eventually.
projects.extended_description: A compact frontend experiment for state updates, event handling, and small-game loop thinking.
highlights: Classic project built for iteration speed

projects.title: toAWS
projects.description: A handful of CLI utilities to make my AWS deployments slightly less painful.
projects.extended_description: A utility-first experiment focused on reducing repetitive deployment friction in personal workflows.
highlights: CLI ergonomics for faster daily usage

projects.title: WalloWords
projects.description: A word-based experiment actively in development.
projects.extended_description: Currently evolving as a lightweight idea sandbox for word mechanics, interaction rhythm, and UI feel.
highlights: Active WIP with frequent direction changes
